sections.paragraph:
i.q. זָכַךְ [fut. יִזְכֶּה], to be pure (always in a moral sense), Job 15:14 25:4 Psa 51:6 Mic 6:11. (Arab. زكا, Syr. ܕܟܐ and ܙܟܐܳ id. The Greek ἅγιος, ἁγνός, and probably also the Lat. sacer, sancio, transp. castus, are from the same stock.)

Piel, to make pure, to cleanse, e.g. the course of life, the soul, Psa 73:13 Pro 20:9. Psa 119:9, בַּמֶּה־יְזַכֶּה נַעַר אֶת־אָרְחֹו “how shall a young man cleanse his way?” i.e. maintain purity of life?

Hithpael הִזַּכָּה for הִתְזַכָּה to cleanse himself, Isa 1:16. [The accent shews that this is not Niph. of זָכַךְ. See Thes.]

[Derivative, זָכוּ.]
